segments.p1-11-g12: Single-word interjection 'Нет' here is an emphatic negation/exclamation ('No') used alone to contradict or reject an idea; repetition right after increases emphasis. It stands outside normal clause structure and does not take a verb.
segments.p1-11-g13: A second repetition of the interjection for emphasis; functionally identical to the first 'Нет', reinforcing the speaker's denial or correction.
segments.p1-11-g14: This whole chunk is the main clause: 'подполье' is the nominative subject (neuter noun) meaning 'the underground' (life/condition), 'во всяком случае' is an adverbial idiom meaning 'in any case' (preposition 'в' appears as 'во' before the cluster), and 'выгоднее' is the comparative form of the adjective 'выгодный' used predicatively ('is more advantageous' / 'preferable'). There is no copula; the comparative expresses a state relative to some unstated alternative.
segments.p1-11-g15: 'Там' is a locative adverb meaning 'there' (indicating place). 'По крайней мере' is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'at least' or 'at the very least' (literally 'by an extreme measure'), and 'можно' is an impersonal modal predicate meaning 'it is possible' / 'one may' — together they form 'There, at least, it is possible.'
segments.p1-11-g18: Interjection 'Эх' expresses a sigh, regret, or exclamation (close to English 'ah' or 'oh') and functions alone to convey the speaker's feeling; it does not contribute grammatical structure to adjacent clauses.
segments.p1-11-g19: This clause contains several small particles and the verb: 'да' is a discourse particle/affirmation ('yes' / 'well') used to introduce or emphasize the remark, 'ведь' is a reinforcing particle meaning 'after all', 'я' is the first-person subject 'I', 'и' here functions as an intensifier 'even/also', 'тут' is an adverb 'here'/'in this situation', and 'вру' is the first-person singular present of 'врать' ('I lie'). Together they mean 'Yes — after all, I even lie here.'
segments.p1-11-g37: То есть is a fixed discourse phrase meaning “that is” or “I mean”; здесь есть appears as part of that phrase rather than as an independent copula. Я is the 1st-person subject. И here is a particle with an emphatic/attendant sense (“even/indeed”), and верю is the 1st-person present indicative of верить (“I believe”) with no explicit object — a predication about the speaker’s attitude.
segments.p1-11-g38: пожалуй is a parenthetical modal particle meaning “perhaps” or “I suppose,” used to soften or hedge the preceding assertion; it does not change the grammatical structure, only the force of the claim.
segments.p1-11-g39: но is the coordinating conjunction “but.” в + то же самое + время is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning “at the very same time” or “simultaneously”; то is the demonstrative “that/the,” же is an emphasizing particle, самое intensifies to “the very,” and время is the noun “time.”
segments.p1-11-g40: неизвестно here is an impersonal predicate meaning “it is unknown” or “I don’t know”; with почему (“why”) the pair forms the idiom “неизвестно почему,” i.e. “for some unknown reason” or “it’s not known why.” Grammatically, this is an impersonal adverbial phrase modifying the following clause.
segments.p1-11-g41: чувствую and подозреваю are two first-person present verbs joined by the coordinating particle и (“and”), meaning “I feel and I suspect.” Both verbs take the subordinate clause introduced by что as their object (they are psychological/state predicates). The order places the verbs in sequence to show two related mental acts.
segments.p1-11-g42: что is the subordinating conjunction “that,” introducing the content clause. Я is the subject, and вру is the 1st-person present of врать (“I lie”). как + сапожник is a simile meaning “like a cobbler/shoemaker” (как introduces manner; сапожник is nominative as the standard of comparison), so the speaker says he lies in the manner of a cobbler.
segments.p1-11-g43: Так is a discourse particle meaning 'so' or 'then' introducing the question. Для чего is a fixed pair meaning 'for what' (i.e. 'for what purpose' / 'why'). Же is an emphatic particle that strengthens the question. Писали is the past plural form of писать with no expressed subject here (indefinite or generic 'they' or 'one wrote'); it asks about the act of writing in the past. Всё это is the nominal phrase 'all this' (всё = 'all', это = 'this') functioning as the object of писали.
segments.p1-11-g44: Говорите is present tense, 2nd person plural (or polite singular) 'you say/are saying'; word order with the verb first gives a brusque or pointed tone. Вы is the 2nd-person pronoun (plural/polite) as subject, and мне is the dative 'to me' marking the addressee of the speaking.
segments.p1-11-g45: А вот is a colloquial attention-getter ('but/suppose now'); посадил is past tense of посадить ('to put/seat' but here 'to imprison'), and with the particle бы it forms the conditional/subjunctive 'I would have imprisoned'. The pronoun я is the subject; вас is the accusative object 'you'. Лет with the preposition на indicates duration ('for forty years'). Безо is a variant of без used before certain words; всякого is genitive singular meaning 'any/any kind of' and governs занятия (genitive) meaning 'occupation' — together 'without any occupation.'
segments.p1-11-g46: Да и is a colloquial linking phrase often adding emphasis ('and besides' / 'and then'); пришел is past tense of прийти 'to come', and with бы here forms the conditional 'would come'. К вам is the dative phrase 'to you', and через сорок лет is the temporal phrase 'in/after forty years' (через used to mark time after which something happens).
segments.p1-11-g47: В is the simple locative preposition 'in/inside'. Подполье here is a noun meaning a basement/underground dwelling or 'the underground' in the literal, physical sense (the narrator's cellar-like room).
segments.p1-11-g48: Наведаться is a perfective verb meaning 'to pay a visit' or 'to drop by' — used here as the verb of purpose/intention (I would come to pay a visit). It is colloquial and slightly brisk in tone.
segments.p1-11-g49: До чего is a colloquial interrogative 'to what (point)/to what end' (до as 'up to/to' + чего 'what'); вы is the subject 'you' and дошли is past plural of дойти 'to reach/arrive' (here rhetorical: 'what have you come to / what have you reached/amounted to'). The whole clause asks what state or result the person would have reached after those forty years.
segments.p1-11-g20: Вру is the 1st-person singular present of врать “to lie.” It is a simple, direct statement (‘I lie’) and the comma signals that the clause will be explained or justified afterward.
segments.p1-11-g21: потому что is the subordinating conjunction meaning “because” (потому ‘for that reason’ + что); сам is an emphatic reflexive/adjective meaning “myself” (emphasizing the subject); знаю is 1sg present of знать “to know.” Together: “because I myself know.”
segments.p1-11-g22: как here introduces a simile/comparison “as” or “like”; дважды два is a short idiomatic expression literally ‘twice two’ (=2×2) used to mean something plain/obvious or certain. The phrase modifies знаю: “I know as surely as two times two.”
segments.p1-11-g23: что begins the subordinate content clause “that…”; вовсе не is an emphatic negation meaning “not at all” (вовсе intensifies the negative не); подполье here refers to the social/psychological ‘Underground’ (not a physical cellar); лучше is the comparative “better” (comparative form of хороший) describing подполье. The clause: “that it is not the Underground that is better.”
segments.p1-11-g24: а is a contrastive conjunction “but/while”; что-то is the indefinite pronoun “something” (что + то = что-то); другое is an adjective/noun “other/another,” so что-то другое = “something else.” This contrasts with ‘the Underground.’
segments.p1-11-g25: совсем is an adverb “completely/entirely” intensifying другое; другое repeats ‘other’ for stronger emphasis: “something completely different.”
segments.p1-11-g26: которого is a genitive singular masculine relative pronoun referring back to ‘something’ (другое) and linking to the transitive feeling verb that takes genitive; я is the subject “I”; жажду is 1sg present of жаждать ‘to long for/ crave’ (here used transitively with genitive). The phrase: “which I long for.”
segments.p1-11-g27: но is a contrastive conjunction “but”; которого repeats the same genitive relative pronoun referring to the same antecedent (‘which’); никак is an adverb meaning “in no way/ by no means” (intensifying the negative); не is the negation; найду is 1sg future of найти “to find” (here: ‘I cannot find’). Together: “but which I can by no means find!”
segments.p1-11-g28: К + noun in dative here forms an idiomatic exclamation meaning 'to hell with ...'. The preposition к normally marks direction ('to/toward'), but in fixed phrases like 'К черту!' it is an imperative expletive; подполье is the object being dismissed. The whole segment is a terse exclamatory sentence with the normal Russian word order (preposition + noun).
segments.p1-11-g29: Даже вот что тут было бы лучше: is a left-dislocated, emphatic clause introducing a hypothetical preference. 'Даже' (even) intensifies; 'вот' is a discourse marker pointing to what follows; 'что' is a relative/introductory 'what/that'; 'тут' ('here/at this point') situates the remark; 'было' is the past tense form used with the particle 'бы' to make a conditional ('would be'); and 'лучше' is the comparative 'better'. The colon marks that an explanatory conditional follows.
segments.p1-11-g30: This clause gives the condition: 'это - если б я верил сам хоть чему-нибудь из всего того,'. 'Это' ('this/it') refers back to the preceding idea; 'если' + short form 'б' marks the conditional ('if ... would'); 'я верил' uses past imperfective to express a hypothetical ongoing belief ('I believed/were to believe'); 'сам' is an emphatic reflexive meaning 'myself'; 'хоть' here means 'at least/even' and combines with 'чему-нибудь' ('to anything', dative) to mean 'in at least anything'; 'из всего того' ('of all that') frames the set from which 'anything' would come. Word order is standard: conditional particle follows 'если' and the main clause of the condition comes after the dash.
segments.p1-11-g31: 'что теперь написал.' completes the conditional and is a relative clause meaning 'that I have now written.' 'что' introduces the content clause; 'теперь' places the writing in the immediate present/past ('now/just'); 'написал' is past tense, here functioning as the perfective past 'wrote/has written' referring to the text just produced. The clause modifies 'всего того' from the previous segment.
segments.p1-11-g32: 'Клянусь же вам,' is a strong verbal formula 'I swear to you.' 'Клянусь' is first-person singular present of 'клясться' meaning 'I swear'; 'же' is an emphatic particle strengthening the assertion; 'вам' is the dative 'to you' (addressing the listeners). This segment functions as the sentence's introductory oath.
segments.p1-11-g33: Господа is a vocative/addressing noun 'gentlemen' (literally 'sirs') placed after the oath to show whom he is addressing; Russian often uses this noun in the nominative as a direct form of address. It’s not grammatically linked by case to the verb here, but pragmatically completes the address.
segments.p1-11-g34: 'что я ни одному,' begins the content clause: 'that I ... not even one ...' 'что' introduces the content of the oath; 'я' is the subject 'I'; 'ни одному' combines the negative particle 'ни' with the dative 'одному' to mean 'not a single one' (used with verbs like 'верю' that take dative for the object of disbelief in this construction). The negation continues in the next segment.
segments.p1-11-g35: 'ни одному-таки словечку не верю из того,' continues the negation and specifies what he does not believe. The repeated 'ни' + 'ни' pattern intensifies 'not even one'; 'таки' is a colloquial/intensive particle emphasizing 'at all/indeed'; 'словечку' is the dative diminutive 'a little word/wordlet' (object of 'верю'); 'не верю' is present-tense negation 'do not believe'; 'из того' ('of that') links back to 'what I have written.' Word order places the negative verb after the emphasized object for rhetorical force.
segments.p1-11-g36: 'что теперь настрочил!' closes the sentence with a relative clause 'that I have just scribbled!' 'что' introduces the clause; 'теперь' ('now/just') locates the action; 'настрочил' (perfective past of 'настрочить') is vivid and colloquial 'scribbled' or 'threw off in writing,' giving a derogatory tone to his own text. The exclamation mark underscores the strong emotional denial.
segments.p1-11-g1: Конец концов is an idiomatic plural/genitive construction literally 'the end of ends' used as a discourse adverb meaning 'after all' or 'in the end'. The first word is the base noun; the second is its genitive plural form that produces the idiom.
segments.p1-11-g2: господа is a vocative/direct address (plural) 'gentlemen' (here used like 'sirs'/'gentlemen'); the colon marks that the speaker is addressing the audience.
segments.p1-11-g3: лучше functions as an impersonal comparative particle meaning 'it is better to' (here introducing a recommendation). ничего is the negative pronoun 'nothing' (genitive/accusative used with negation). не + делать is the negated infinitive 'to do nothing.' The whole phrase is an exclamatory/elliptical clause meaning 'it's better to do nothing.'
segments.p1-11-g4: This is an elliptical exclamation: Лучше functions as 'better' (implying 'it is better to have/choose'). сознательная инерция is a nominative noun phrase ('deliberate/conscious inertia') with adjective agreeing in gender and number; together they form the predicate/content of the exclamation.
segments.p1-11-g5: Итак is a discourse connective meaning 'so' / 'now then' that signals a concluding or summative move in the speech.
segments.p1-11-g6: да здравствует is an optative/exclamatory formula meaning 'long live' where да is a particle that gives an old-fashioned or emphatic wish ('may/let') and здравствует is the 3rd-person present form used idiomatically. подполье is the nominative subject 'the underground' (here the figurative 'underground' rather than a cellar).
segments.p1-11-g7: Я 'I' is the subject. хоть и is a concessive conjunction 'even though/although' and сказа́л is the past-tense masculine form 'I said', introducing a concessive clause ('even though I said...').
segments.p1-11-g8: что is the complementizer 'that' introducing the content clause. завидую is 1st-person present 'I envy.' нормальному человеку is dative singular noun phrase 'to/for a normal person' (нормальному agrees in case and number). до + последней желчи is a measure phrase 'to the last drop of bile'—до marks the limit and последней желчи is genitive indicating extent; here желчи is used metaphorically as 'bitterness/spite.'
segments.p1-11-g9: но is the coordinating conjunction 'but'. на таких условиях is a prepositional phrase meaning 'under/on such conditions' with на + prepositional plural (условиях) and таких agreeing in number/case.
segments.p1-11-g10: в каких is a relative prepositional phrase 'in which' (в + relative pronoun какие in gen./prep. form), я вижу его is the relative clause 'I see him' where вижу is present 'I see' and его is the object 'him' referring back to 'нормальному человеку.' The clause modifies 'условиях.'
segments.p1-11-g11: не хочу им быть is 'I do not want to be him' where не negates хочу 'I want', им is an instrumental pronoun used as predicate complement 'to be him', and быть is the infinitive 'to be'. The parenthetical clause starting with хотя 'although' contains всё-таки (emphatic 'still/after all'), перестану (perfective future 'I will cease/stop') with ему завидовать ('to envy him' — завидовать takes the dative).
segments.p1-11-g50: This is an impersonal question built with можно + infinitive (можно оставлять) expressing possibility; Разве at the start makes it a rhetorical question expecting denial. Человека is the direct object in accusative, одного is an adjective-like accusative agreeing with человека meaning 'alone' or 'by himself', and на сорок лет is a duration phrase (на + accusative of years). Оставлять is imperfective 'to leave/abandon', here meaning 'to leave (somebody) in a state' for that period.
segments.p1-11-g51: И это не стыдно is a simple declarative clause where это (neuter demonstrative pronoun) functions as the subject and стыдно is a predicative adjective/adverbial predicate; Russian omits the present-tense copula 'to be', so 'не стыдно' = 'is not shameful'. The initial И adds connective/emphatic force ('and'/'even').
segments.p1-11-g52: This repeats the same structure: и это не унизительно, where унизительно is another predicative adjective meaning 'humiliating'; the clause is exclamatory and the И links it to the previous remark (can be read as 'and/indeed').
segments.p1-11-g53: Может быть is a parenthetical expression meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be' and functions adverbially to hedge the upcoming statement; grammatically it's a fixed phrase composed of может (possibility) + быть ('to be').
segments.p1-11-g54: Скажете вы мне is future tense (сказать, perfective) 2nd person plural/formal (скажете) with the pronoun вы placed after the verb for neutral or slightly emphatic word order; мне is the dative indirect object 'to me'.
segments.p1-11-g55: Презрительно покачивая головами is a gerundial participial phrase (деепричастие покачивая) expressing a simultaneous action 'while/with shaking your heads'; презрительно is an adverb modifying the gerund, and головами is the instrumental plural of голова indicating the means or part used ('with your heads' = by shaking your heads).
segments.p1-11-g56: This single clause contains two coordinated parts. Вы жаждете жизни uses жаждете (present 2nd person plural of жаждать) with the genitive жизни to mean 'you long for/are thirsty for life'. И сами разрешаете жизненные вопросы uses сами for emphasis ('you yourselves') and разрешаете (present 2nd person plural of разрешать) meaning 'resolve/settle' here; логической путаницей is instrumental (feminine singular) indicating manner or means — 'by a logical confusion' or 'with a muddle of logic'.
segments.p1-11-g90: Вы is the 2nd-person plural/formal pronoun. Хвалитесь is the present tense, 2nd-person plural of the reflexive verb хвалиться 'to boast, to pride oneself', and takes an instrumental object. Сознанием is in the instrumental case, the thing you 'boast of' (literally 'you boast with/by consciousness').
segments.p1-11-g91: Но introduces a contrast. Только here means 'only/merely' and restricts the following verb. Колеблетесь is present tense 2nd-person plural of the reflexive verb колебаться, meaning 'hesitate' or 'waver' (i.e. you merely hesitate rather than act).
segments.p1-11-g92: Потому что is the causal conjunction 'because/for'. Хоть ... и forms a concessive clause ('though/even though'); here хотя/хоть + и adds the sense 'even though' or 'although'. Ум у вас literally places ум 'mind' with possession marked by у вас ('you have a mind'), and работает is 3rd-person singular present 'works/functions'. The phrase says your mind does work despite what follows.
segments.p1-11-g93: Но again contrasts with the previous clause. Сердце ваше ('your heart') places the noun before the possessive for emphasis. Развратом is instrumental and names the cause or means ('by/with depravity'), while помрачено is a short-form passive/predicative (neuter to agree with сердце) meaning 'is darkened' or 'is clouded'.
segments.p1-11-g94: А connects/contrasts the final point. Без governs the genitive: 'without'. Чистого сердца are adjectives and noun in the genitive ('of a pure heart'). Полного is another adjective in the genitive that further qualifies or intensifies 'чистого сердца'; the dash signals an explanatory or amplifying pause (roughly 'a pure — complete — heart').
segments.p1-11-g95: Правильного сознания is in the genitive and names what will be lacking. Не будет is the impersonal negative existential construction using the future of быть ('there will not be'); together they mean 'there will be no proper consciousness.'
segments.p1-11-g96: И is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and' that continues the previous thought. Сколько + noun in the genitive (назойливости) expresses an exclamatory quantity ('how much/of what an amount'); в вас uses the preposition в + accusative/locative pronoun (вас here functions as the object of the preposition) to mean 'in you.' Назойливости is in the singular genitive after сколько, meaning 'obtrusiveness/insistence.'
segments.p1-11-g97: Как here is an exclamatory 'how' introducing an emotional comment. Напрашиваетесь is a present tense, 2nd person plural reflexive verb (напрашиваться), meaning 'to impose oneself' or 'to solicit attention' — the -ся marks it as reflexive/impersonal in sense. Вы is the 2nd-person plural/formal pronoun and agrees with the verb form.
segments.p1-11-g98: Как again is exclamatory 'how.' Кривляетесь is present tense, 2nd person plural of кривляться (reflexive) and denotes making faces or putting on an affected expression; the exclamation mark intensifies the reproach. Вы is the addressed pronoun agreeing with the verb form.
segments.p1-11-g99: One-word sentence used as an interjection: Ложь in the nominative (same form as accusative for inanimate nouns) is used as an exclamatory accusation 'a lie' or 'falsehood.' The comma marks the repeated, staccato rhythm.
segments.p1-11-g100: Repetition for emphasis: ложь (again 'lie/falsehood') joined by the coordinating conjunction и ('and') — here simply linking the repeated noun to intensify the accusation. The final exclamation mark makes the line an emphatic denunciation.
segments.p1-11-g101: Разумеется is a sentence-initial modal adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally,' signaling the speaker's stance toward the following statement. It functions as a discourse marker and does not change form.
segments.p1-11-g102: This clause places the object (все эти ваши слова — 'all these your words') before the subject for emphasis. Все эти ('all these') is a common determiner sequence; ваши modifies слова ('words/utterances'). Я is the subject 'I,' сам is an emphatic reflexive/adjective 'myself' stressing the subject's personal role, теперь is the adverb 'now,' and сочинил is the past tense, perfective verb (masculine singular form) meaning 'composed' or 'invented.' The verb at the end is normal in Russian for emphasis/closure.
segments.p1-11-g111: 'Но' is a coordinating conjunction meaning 'but' that introduces a contrasting or incredulous remark; 'неужели' is an interrogative particle expressing strong disbelief ('really? / surely?') and here functions as an interjection, set off by a comma to heighten the speaker's astonishment.
segments.p1-11-g112: This whole phrase is the main clause's predicate: the repeated 'неужели' continues the rhetorical question; 'вы' is the (formal/plural) subject; 'и' here adds emphasis ('even/also'); 'в самом деле' is an idiom meaning 'really/in truth'; 'до того' ('до' + demonstrative 'того') forms a degree phrase 'to such an extent'; and 'легковесны' is a predicative adjective (plural agreeing with 'вы') meaning 'frivolous' — note there is no copula in present-tense Russian, so the adjective functions as the verb 'are'.
segments.p1-11-g113: 'Что' here is a complementizer introducing the content clause ('that ...'); it does not mean 'what' but 'that'. 'Воображаете' is 2nd person plural present indicative of 'воображать' ('to imagine/fancy'), and the subject 'вы' is carried over from the previous clause rather than repeated, so the verb stands alone as the predicate of this subordinate clause.
segments.p1-11-g114: 'Будто' introduces the reported/hypothetical content ('as if' / 'that'), followed by the explicit subject 'я' and the object phrase 'это все' ('all this'). 'Напечатаю' is the perfective simple future (1st person singular) — 'I will print/publish.' The coordinating particle 'да' here functions as an archaic/colloquial 'and' ('and moreover'), 'еще' strengthens it ('even/moreover'), and 'вам дам читать' is the construction 'дать + dative + infinitive' meaning 'to let/allow someone to read' (future 'дам' = 'I will give/allow', 'читать' = 'to read').
segments.p1-11-g115: An introductory presentational clause: И вот ещё для меня задача literally 'And here (is) another task for me.' The verb 'to be' is omitted (typical in Russian), so задача (nominative) is the main noun; для меня is a genitive-phrase marking whose problem it is; вот is a presentational particle ('here/there is').
segments.p1-11-g116: Для чего is a fixed interrogative adverbial phrase meaning 'for what purpose/why'. Note that после предлога для the interrogative takes the genitive (чего).
segments.p1-11-g117: В самом деле is an idiomatic parenthetical meaning 'in fact/really' (в + prepositional сам + дело); grammatically it's a prepositional phrase used adverbially to confirm or soften the question.
segments.p1-11-g118: Называю is the 1st-person singular present of называть 'to call/name'; Russian uses the instrumental after называть when giving someone a name or description (называть кого кем), so господами is instrumental plural ('as gentlemen'). The pronoun я follows the verb for emphasis (inversion).
segments.p1-11-g119: Repeated како́го рода question: для чего + verb обращаюсь ('I address/appeal') — обращаюсь is reflexive (обращаться) meaning 'to address oneself to' and takes к + dative (к вам). Word order places the purpose question before the verb for rhetorical effect.
segments.p1-11-g120: Как будто introduces a comparative subordinate clause 'as if'; и here is emphatic ('even/indeed'), and вправду is an adverb 'truly/actually'. К читателям is dative plural 'to the readers' showing the addressee of обращаюсь within the comparative clause.
segments.p1-11-g121: Таких is the plural genitive/demonstrative form of такой and agrees with признаний; together Таких признаний means 'confessions of such a kind' or 'such confessions.' The phrase stands in front of the clause as the thing being talked about and is set off by a comma.
segments.p1-11-g122: Какие begins a relative clause referring back to 'Таких признаний' (literally 'which I intend...'). Я намерен uses the short-form adjective/participle намерен as a predicative 'I intend' (no auxiliary verb). Начать (perfective infinitive) + излагать (imperfective infinitive) form a common combination meaning 'to begin to set forth / to start stating' — the first verb marks the initiation of the ongoing action expressed by the second.
segments.p1-11-g123: Не печатают is a generic third-person plural assertion 'they do not print' (the subject is indefinite/general). И here carries the sense 'also / even' connecting the second clause. Другим is dative plural ('to/for others'); читать не дают is an idiomatic causative/allowing construction literally 'do not give to read' = 'do not allow to be read' — so the whole clause says they neither print these confessions nor permit others to read them.
segments.p1-11-g124: По крайней мере is a fixed idiomatic expression meaning 'at least' or 'in any case.' Grammatically it is по + dative (крайней мере), but here it functions as a sentence adverbial rather than a literal spatial/temporal phrase.
segments.p1-11-g125: Я — subject 'I.' Настолько is an adverb of degree 'to such an extent/so.' Твердости is genitive (твердость) used to express an amount/degree that the speaker lacks ('I do not have such firmness'); in constructions of absence Russian often uses the genitive. В себе ('in myself') locates that quality internally. Не имею is present tense first singular 'I do not have.' Да и is a colloquial connective meaning 'and moreover/besides.' Нужным не считаю иметь is the pattern 'считать нужным (inf.)' — 'to consider (something) necessary' — here negated: 'I do not consider it necessary to have (it),' with the infinitive иметь as the thing being judged necessary.
segments.p1-11-g57: И is the simple coordinating 'and'. Как introduces an exclamatory phrase meaning 'how' and precedes a short-form predicate adjective назойливы ('annoying/persistent') used predicatively; there is no copular verb in present-tense Russian, so the adjective stands for 'are annoying'.
segments.p1-11-g58: Как again works as an exclamatory 'how'. Дерзки is a short-form adjective used predicatively ('are bold/insolent'), and ваши выходки is the noun phrase 'your antics' in the nominative; word order places the adjective before the possessor phrase for emphasis.
segments.p1-11-g59: И в то же время is a fixed phrase meaning 'and at the same time'; the following как introduces another exclamatory clause. Вы боитесь is present-tense, second-person plural ('you are afraid'), so the whole clause reads 'and at the same time how you are afraid!'.
segments.p1-11-g60: Вы говорите is present-tense second-person plural 'you say/you speak'. Вздор is a noun 'nonsense' functioning as the direct object; довольны is a short-form adjective meaning 'are satisfied', and им refers back to that nonsense (literally 'with it' / 'about it').
segments.p1-11-g61: Again вы говорите is 'you say'. Дерзости here is a plural noun meaning 'insolences' or 'bold remarks' and functions as the object of говорить; the clause is an assertion about what you say.
segments.p1-11-g62: А introduces a contrast ('but/while'). Сами is emphatic 'yourselves'. Беспрерывно is an adverb 'continuously', and боитесь за них uses the preposition за + pronoun meaning 'are afraid for them' (concerned on their behalf). Просите is present-tense 'you ask/request' and извинения is the noun 'an apology' (so 'you ask for apologies').
segments.p1-11-g63: Вы уверяете is present-tense second-person plural 'you assure' or 'you assert' and serves as the matrix verb introducing the subordinate clause that follows.
segments.p1-11-g64: Что introduces the subordinate clause 'that'. Ничего не боитесь is a negative construction ('you are not afraid of anything' / 'you fear nothing'), with не negating the verb боитесь and ничего as the negative pronoun.
segments.p1-11-g65: И в то же время is again 'and at the same time'. В нашем мнении is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in our opinion' (в + locative), and заискиваете is present 'you fawn/ingratiate yourselves', giving the speaker's judgment.
segments.p1-11-g66: Вы уверяете repeats the present-tense assertion 'you assure/you claim' and introduces the following clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-11-g67: Что introduces the content clause. Скрежещете is present-tense second-person plural of 'скрежетать' and here means 'you gnash/grind' (teeth); зубами is an instrumental plural 'with your teeth' — the usual collocation for 'gnash one's teeth'.
segments.p1-11-g68: И в то же время 'and at the same time' again contrasts the supposed emotion with the action. Острите is present-tense second-person plural of остро́ить (colloquial) meaning 'you make witticisms/quip' — i.e., you are joking sharply.
segments.p1-11-g69: Чтоб is the colloquial/contracted form of чтобы and introduces a purpose clause 'so as to'. Нас is the accusative 'us', and рассмешить is a perfective infinitive 'to make (someone) laugh' — 'to make us laugh'.
segments.p1-11-g103: Это is a demonstrative pronoun introducing the topic ('that/this'); тоже is an adverb meaning 'also/too' modifying the whole statement; из is a preposition requiring the genitive; подполья is the genitive singular of подполье, here used figuratively as 'the underground' (the subterranean/underground sphere).
segments.p1-11-g104: Я is the nominative personal pronoun 'I'; там is an adverb of place 'there' locating the action; сорок лет сряду is a duration expression ('forty years in succession/running') where лет is the genitive plural of год and сряду is an adverb meaning 'in a row'; к governs the dative and introduces the indirect object 'to/listening to'.
segments.p1-11-g105: Я is the nominative 'I'; их is the accusative plural pronoun 'them' (object of выдумал); сам is an emphatic reflexive/ intensive ('myself'), placed after the object for emphasis; выдумал is past tense, perfective, masculine singular of выдумать meaning 'made up' or 'invented' (completed action).
segments.p1-11-g106: ведь is a discourse particle 'after all/you see' giving a reason; только is a restrictor 'only/just'; это is the demonstrative 'this' serving as the limited subject; и here is a small emphatic particle (common in colloquial/dated Russian) reinforcing exclusivity ('indeed/even'); выдумывалось is the reflexive/impartial imperfective past ('was being made up' / 'kept being made up'), indicating repeated or habitual passive-like occurrence.
segments.p1-11-g107: Не negates the following predicate; мудрено is an impersonal/adverbial form meaning roughly 'wonderful/wise' in older usage—here in the fixed phrase Не мудрено = 'it is not surprising' or 'no wonder'.
segments.p1-11-g108: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the clause explaining why it's not surprising; наизусть is an adverb meaning 'by heart'; заучилось is the reflexive perfective (or resultative) past 'was learned by heart' (became learned); и is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking two results; литературную форму is the accusative object 'a literary form' (литературную modifying форму); приняло is past neuter of принять used impersonally here meaning 'took on' or 'assumed' that form (‘it took on a literary form’), with the neuter subject implied (the preceding idea/phrase).
segments.p1-11-g198: A simple comma punctuation mark separating the preceding clause from the following abbreviation; here it functions as the comma in the printed abbreviation “и т. д.” (i.e., it belongs to the phrase “and so on”).
segments.p1-11-g199: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. It begins the standard abbreviated phrase 'и т. д.' (и так далее) meaning 'and so on'; the following 'т.' and 'д.' complete that abbreviation.
segments.p1-11-g200: The single-letter abbreviation 'д.' completes the printed abbreviation 'и т. д.' standing for 'далее' in the phrase 'и так далее' ('and so on'). It's an orthographic abbreviation rather than a full word.
segments.p1-11-g202: К чему is the preposition к + dative pronoun (чему) meaning 'to what' or 'about what'; вы is the 2nd-person plural/formal pronoun 'you'; объясняетесь is the 2nd-person plural present reflexive form of объясняться ('to explain oneself'), so the whole question asks why/what you are explaining yourself about. Note к requires the dative (чему) and объясняетесь includes the reflexive -ся.
segments.p1-11-g203: К чему (к + чему) = 'to/for what'; извиняетесь is the 2nd-person plural present reflexive of извиняться, meaning 'to apologize' or 'to excuse oneself' (here 'apologize for'). The phrase is a direct question asking the reason/ground for the apology.
segments.p1-11-g126: 'Но' is the coordinating conjunction 'but' introducing a remark. 'Видите ли' is a colloquial address meaning 'you see' or 'you understand' — 'видите' is 2nd-person plural present (used here idiomatically as 'you see') and 'ли' is the enclitic/question particle that softens/rhetorically frames the statement.
segments.p1-11-g127: This is a short existential/experiential phrase: 'мне' is the dative 'to me'; 'в' is the preposition introducing direction into the noun; 'голову' is the accusative of 'голова' used idiomatically as 'to (one's) head/mind'; 'пришла' is the past tense feminine of прийти used in the idiom 'пришла в голову' = 'occurred to (one)'; 'фантазия' is the noun 'fantasy/fancy' (here a single, passing whim).
segments.p1-11-g128: 'И' links the clauses ('and'). 'Я' is the subject 'I'. The phrase 'во что бы ни стало' is an idiomatic concessive expression meaning 'no matter what (it costs / happens)'; grammatically it contains the preposition variant 'во' + relative 'что' + conditional particle 'бы' + negative/intensifying particle 'ни' + past-form 'стало' — together yielding 'whatever it takes / no matter what happens'. 'Ее' is the accusative pronoun referring back to 'фантазия' (it), 'хочу' is present tense 'I want', and 'осуществить' is the infinitive 'to carry out / to realize' (what I want to do).
segments.p1-11-g129: 'Вот' is a demonstrative particle ('here/this is' or 'there') used to present or emphasize what follows; 'в' is the preposition 'in'; 'чем' is the instrumental/locative form of 'что' meaning 'what' in the phrase 'в чем (дело)' = 'in what (the matter is)'; 'дело' is 'matter/point' — together 'Вот в чем дело.' = 'This is the point' or 'That's the matter.'
segments.p1-11-g130: 'Есть' here functions existentially ('there are'); 'в' plus the locative plural 'воспоминаниях' means 'in the memories'; 'всякого человека' is a genitive phrase 'of every person' (literally 'every person's'); 'такие вещи' means 'such things' or 'certain things' — together: 'There are, in every person's memories, such things...'.
segments.p1-11-g131: A relative clause introduced by 'которые' ('which/that'). 'Он' is the impersonal/indefinite 'he/one' (the person just mentioned); 'открывает' is present-tense 'opens/reveals' (here 'reveals' secrets); 'не' negates the verb; 'всем' is dative/indirect object 'to everyone' — 'which he does not reveal to everyone.'
segments.p1-11-g132: 'А' here contrasts ('but'); 'разве' is a particle giving a tentative or rhetorical flavor ('perhaps/after all' or 'is it that...'); 'только' means 'only'; 'друзьям' is dative plural 'to friends' — together: 'but (perhaps) only to friends.'
segments.p1-11-g133: 'Есть' again marks existence ('there are'); 'и' here is emphatic ('also/even'); 'такие' means 'such (ones)' — 'There are even such (things).' This sets up the stronger category that follows.
segments.p1-11-g134: Relative clause: 'которые' = 'which/that'; 'он' = 'he/one'; the particle 'и' before 'друзьям' adds emphasis meaning 'even' ('not even to friends'); 'друзьям' is dative 'to friends'; 'не' negates the verb; 'откроет' is future or present-future perfective of 'открыть' meaning 'will reveal / reveal' — here 'will not reveal'.
segments.p1-11-g135: 'А' contrasts ('but'); 'разве' again gives a tentative/rhetorical sense ('perhaps/only'); 'только' is 'only'; 'себе' is dative reflexive 'to himself'; 'самому' intensifies 'сам' ('himself/very self') — 'but perhaps only to himself.'
segments.p1-11-g136: 'Да и то' is a concessive phrase meaning 'and even that' or 'even then'; 'да' here functions like 'and/even', 'и' is connective 'also/even', 'то' points back to the preceding idea ('that (even)'); 'под' with instrumental 'секретом' means 'under secrecy' or 'in secret' — 'and even then, in secret.'
segments.p1-11-g204: This is a colloquial exclamatory cluster: А (contrastive particle) + вот (attention particle, 'well/now') + поди (an old/colloquial imperative-like exclamation meaning 'just try/see') + же (emphatic particle). Together they form a brusque interjection like 'Well now, just you try that' or 'there you are!'.
segments.p1-11-g205: Отвечаю is present-tense 1st person singular of отвечать ('I answer'); here it's a parenthetical reporting clause. Я is the explicit subject placed after the verb for mildly emphatic or colloquial effect.
segments.p1-11-g206: Тут is an adverb meaning 'here' or 'at this point', marking the location or point in the discourse.
segments.p1-11-g207: Впрочем is a discourse particle meaning 'however' or 'mind you', used to qualify or soften the preceding remark.
segments.p1-11-g208: Целая is the adjective целый in feminine form agreeing with психология and here has an emphatic force 'a whole/entire'; психология is the noun 'psychology' (meaning either the science or a complex of motives), so the phrase idiomatically means 'there is a whole psychology to this'.
segments.p1-11-g209: Может быть is a fixed modal phrase meaning 'it may be' or 'perhaps'; the first word (может) expresses possibility and быть is the infinitive 'to be' completing the idiom.
segments.p1-11-g210: И то here functions as a linking phrase 'and/or also that', introducing another possible explanation; и has the sense 'also' and то is the demonstrative/pronominal 'that'.
segments.p1-11-g211: Что introduces the content clause 'that I am simply a coward'; я is the subject, просто is an adverb 'simply/merely' modifying трус, which is a noun meaning 'coward'. Word order is the neutral Russian order: conjunction + subject + modifiers + predicate noun.
segments.p1-11-g212: А может быть is another variant of the modal phrase meaning 'or perhaps' or 'and maybe'; А is a contrastive/softening particle and может быть is the 'might/maybe' construction.
segments.p1-11-g213: И то again signals 'also/that too', introducing an additional possible reason; and functions similarly to the previous sentence's 'и то'.
segments.p1-11-g214: Что introduces the subordinate clause 'that I deliberately imagine an audience before me'. Я is the subject; нарочно is an adverb 'deliberately'; воображаю is present 1st person 'I imagine/ picture'; перед собой is a prepositional phrase literally 'in front of myself' (собой is the reflexive form of себя used after перед) indicating the imagined position; публику is the accusative object 'an audience/the public'.
segments.p1-11-g215: Чтоб is the colloquial contraction of чтобы, meaning 'in order that / so that' and is followed by the infinitive вести; вести себя is a fixed reflexive phrase 'to behave oneself' (here вести takes себя with себя after it), and приличнее is the comparative form meaning 'more decently/more properly'—used adverbially to indicate manner.
segments.p1-11-g216: В то время когда is a temporal connector 'at the time when / while'; буду записывать is the analytic future (буду + imperfective infinitive) meaning 'I will be writing / I will be recording'—it emphasizes the ongoing action in the future while the speaker imagines the audience.
segments.p1-11-g70: Вы знаете is a straightforward 2nd-person plural present indicative: Вы (you, polite/plural) is the subject and знаете (from знать) means 'know' or 'are aware'. The comma flags the coming subordinate clause introduced by что.
segments.p1-11-g71: Что introduces a subordinate clause ('that'). Остроты (plural) here means 'witticisms' or 'quips' (the mental/sharpness sense) with the possessive ваши following it. Неостроумны is a short-form plural adjective with a negative prefix (не-) and serves as the predicate: 'are not witty/unfunny.'
segments.p1-11-g72: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but' introducing the contrast; вы repeats the subject 'you' (2nd-person plural/formal). The comma gives a pause before the parenthetical очевидно.
segments.p1-11-g73: Очевидно is an adverbial modal comment ('obviously/apparently') modifying the coming predicate; it stands as a sentence adverb and is set off by commas.
segments.p1-11-g74: Довольны is a short-form plural adjective meaning 'satisfied' and takes its object in the instrumental case (здесь литературным достоинством). Их is the possessive 'their' referring back to the witticisms; литературным достоинством is instrumental ('with/by their literary merit'). Очень simply intensifies the adjective.
segments.p1-11-g75: Вам is the dative pronoun 'to you' (marks the experiencer in constructions like 'it happened to you'); the comma sets off the following modal phrase.
segments.p1-11-g76: Может быть is a fused modal phrase equivalent to English 'perhaps' or 'may be' (мочь + быть here build the idiomatic 'might/maybe'). It is parenthetical and modifies the whole predicate that follows.
segments.p1-11-g77: Действительно here means 'indeed/actually.' Случалось is the past tense, impersonal/habitual form of случаться used with a dative experiencer (Вам) plus an infinitive: случалось страдать = 'it happened (to you) to suffer' / 'you happened to suffer'. Страдать is the infinitive 'to suffer.'
segments.p1-11-g78: Нисколько не is an emphatic negation meaning 'not at all' (нисколько before не): вы (you) + уважаете (present 2nd pl. 'respect/esteem') takes the object своего страдания. Своего is the reflexive possessive 'your own' and страдания is genitive singular 'suffering' (after the negation the object is presented with genitive).
segments.p1-11-g79: В вас есть is an existential construction 'there is in you' (literally 'in you there is'), with и functioning here as 'also/even' to stress the presence of правда ('truth'). Есть is present 'there is/exists.'
segments.p1-11-g80: Но introduces the contrast; в вас нет is the negative existential 'there is no ... in you' and требует (requires) the genitive. Целомудрия is the genitive of 'целомудрие' meaning chastity/moral purity.
segments.p1-11-g81: Вы is the subject; из самого мелкого тщеславия is a prepositional phrase with из + genitive meaning 'out of/from the very smallest/pettiest vanity' (самого + мелкого intensifies the smallness). Несете is present 2nd-person-plural 'you carry/bring' and правду is the direct object 'the truth.'
segments.p1-11-g82: На позор is an adverbial phrase with на indicating purpose/result: 'to shame' or 'for disgrace' (на + noun expresses the outcome or aim). It continues the series of adverbial destinations introduced after несете правду.
segments.p1-11-g83: На рынок uses на + accusative to indicate direction/toward (literally 'to the market'); here it is figurative — 'to the marketplace' meaning 'to public view' — and completes the triad of purposes.
segments.p1-11-g86: Вы хотите + infinitive (сказать) is the present-tense construction 'you want to say.' Действительно is an adverb 'really/indeed' intensifying the desire. Что-то is the indefinite pronoun 'something' formed from что + то (что = 'what', то = the particle forming indefiniteness).
segments.p1-11-g87: Но из боязни is a prepositional phrase with из + genitive meaning 'out of/from fear'; прячете is present 2nd-person-plural 'you hide/conceal' and ваше последнее слово is the direct object: ваше = 'your', последнее = 'last/final', слово = 'word/utterance' (here 'last word').
segments.p1-11-g88: Потому что introduces the causal clause 'because.' У вас нет is the possessive existentials 'you do not have' (у + dative + нет + genitive noun), решимости is genitive 'of/any determination' and его (here the object pronoun) refers back to 'your last word' and is the object of высказать (infinitive 'to express/voice').
segments.p1-11-g89: А contrasts with the previous clause ('but/and'), and только limits what you have to 'merely' or 'only.' Трусливое (adjective) + нахальство (noun) form a nominal predicate: 'only cowardly impudence' — what you possess instead of resolve.
segments.p1-11-g172: The clause begins with the pronoun subject 'Я' followed by the emphatic particle 'же' (adds contrast/emphasis). 'Пишу' is present tense, first person singular (I write). 'Для одного себя' is a reflexive, emphatic phrase meaning 'for oneself alone' (literally 'for one self'), and 'и раз навсегда объявляю' is a performative present 'and I declare once and for all' — 'раз' = 'once' and 'навсегда' = 'forever', with 'объявляю' in the present used to make a decisive statement.
segments.p1-11-g173: 'что' introduces the content clause ('that'). 'Если' = 'if'; the particle 'и' after 'я' has a concessive sense here ('even' — 'even if I write'). 'Пишу' repeats the present-tense verb. 'Как бы' is a modal/hedging construction meaning 'as if' (here roughly 'as though'), and 'обращаясь' is a present gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'addressing' (simultaneous action), governing the dative 'к читателям' ('to the readers').
segments.p1-11-g174: The correlative 'то' (then/so) follows the conditional clause. 'Единственно только' is a doubled intensifier meaning 'solely/only'. 'Для показу' is a set phrase meaning 'for show' — 'показу' is used in the genitive after 'для' and has a colloquial/idiomatic feel.
segments.p1-11-g175: 'Потому что' is the causal conjunction 'because'. 'Так' ('that way') refers back to the foregoing (the 'for show' behaviour). 'Мне легче' uses the dative pronoun 'мне' with the comparative adjective 'легче' to mean 'it is easier for me', and the infinitive 'писать' completes the clause ('to write').
segments.p1-11-g176: 'Тут' is an adverb meaning 'here' / 'at this point' and is placed before the noun; 'форма' is nominative and the copula 'is' is omitted (zero-copula), so the phrase amounts to 'Here (is) a form' or 'This is a form.'
segments.p1-11-g177: 'Одна' agrees with the feminine noun 'форма' and here functions as an intensifier meaning 'mere/just'; 'пустая' is the predicate adjective 'empty' modifying 'форма' — together 'a mere empty form.'
segments.p1-11-g178: This is an existential construction: 'читателей' is genitive plural ('readers') governed by the negated impersonal verb 'не будет' ('there will not be'); 'у меня' marks possession/source ('for me' / 'I will not have'), and the particle 'же' adds contrast/emphasis ('as for readers' / 'but readers... never').
segments.p1-11-g179: 'Я' is the subject; 'уже' is the adverb 'already'; 'объявил' is past tense, masculine singular (I declared/announced) — a completed action; 'это' is the direct object ('this'/'that'), referring back to the preceding statement.
segments.p1-11-g182: 'Я' is subject; 'ничем' is instrumental used with negation ('ничем не стесняться') meaning 'by nothing' → 'not at all' / 'by nothing do I wish to be embarrassed or constrained.' 'Не хочу' is present tense 'I do not want'; 'стесняться' is the reflexive infinitive 'to be embarrassed/hesitate' or 'to restrain oneself'. 'В редакции моих записок' is a prepositional phrase indicating the sphere ('in the editing/edition of my notes'), with 'редакции' understood as 'edition/editing' and 'моих записок' in the genitive.
segments.p1-11-g154: Замечу is the 1st-person singular future (perfective) form of заметить used parenthetically as 'I’ll note / I remark'. Кстати is an adverb meaning 'incidentally / by the way' and here marks the clause as a brief incidental remark.
segments.p1-11-g155: Гейне is the proper name 'Heine' in nominative (no article in Russian). Утверждает is present tense, 3rd-person singular of the imperfective verb утверждать, meaning 'asserts/maintains' — it presents a general claim by Heine.
segments.p1-11-g156: Что introduces a subordinate clause 'that ...'. Верные автобиографии is the plural subject ('faithful/autonomous autobiographies'), and почти is an adverb modifying невозможны. Невозможны is the short-form plural adjective used predicatively: 'are impossible (almost)'.
segments.p1-11-g157: И connects this clause to the previous one. Человек сам is the nominative subject with сам adding emphasis ('the person himself'). Об себе is 'about himself' (об + prepositional of the reflexive pronoun), наверно is a modal adverb 'probably', and налжет is 3rd-person singular future/predictive form meaning 'will lie' (here used as a prediction about what a person will do).
segments.p1-11-g158: По его мнению is a fixed expression meaning 'according to his opinion / in his view'; по + dative (его мнению) marks whose opinion it is. Его here functions as the possessive/genitive form referring to 'him'.
segments.p1-11-g159: Руссо is the proper name 'Rousseau' in nominative; commas set it off as the referent being discussed.
segments.p1-11-g160: Например is an adverbial parenthetical 'for example', used to introduce an illustrative case.
segments.p1-11-g161: Непременно is an adverb meaning 'certainly/undoubtedly' and modifies the past-tense налгал ('lied'), which is masculine singular past of налгать. На себя uses на + accusative (себя) to mean 'about himself' (i.e., directed at himself), and в своей исповеди is a locative/prepositional phrase 'in his confession', where своей is the reflexive possessive matching the subject.
segments.p1-11-g162: И даже adds emphasis ('and even'); умышленно is an adverb 'deliberately' modifying the repeated past налгал, which reinforces that the lying was intentional.
segments.p1-11-g163: Из + genitive expresses motive 'out of / from' (из тщеславия = 'out of vanity'), where тщеславия is the genitive of the noun 'vanity'.
segments.p1-11-g164: Я is the nominative pronoun 'I'. Уверен is a short-form adjective functioning as a predicate meaning 'am sure' or 'confident' and agrees with the subject.
segments.p1-11-g165: Что begins a subordinate clause 'that ...'. Гейне is the proper name 'Heine' and прав is the short-form adjective 'right' used predicatively: 'Heine is right.'
segments.p1-11-g166: Я очень хорошо понимаю is a main-clause statement 'I understand very well' (понимаю is 1st-person singular present of понимать; очень хорошо is an adverbial intensifier).
segments.p1-11-g167: Как introduces an indirect question 'how' or 'in what way'. Иногда 'sometimes'; можно is impersonal/modal 'it is possible / one can'. Единственно 'only/merely' limits the cause; из + genitive (тщеславия) indicates motive 'out of vanity'. Наклепать is a perfective infinitive 'to fabricate/slander', and на себя ('against/about oneself') is the target (на + accusative). Целые преступления is the direct object in accusative plural ('whole crimes' i.e. major offenses attributed to oneself).
segments.p1-11-g168: И даже is emphatic 'and even'; очень хорошо again intensifies 'I grasp very well'. Постигаю is 1st-person singular present of постигать here meaning 'I comprehend / grasp' (a stronger nuance than понимать).
segments.p1-11-g169: Какого рода is a fixed phrase meaning 'what kind of' (какого modifies рода). Может быть is a modal phrase 'may be / can be' expressing possibility; это тщеславие is the noun phrase 'this vanity' as the subject/predicate of that possibility.
segments.p1-11-g170: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however' placed at the sentence start. Гейне is a proper name in the nominative (Heine) and is the subject; судил is the past tense, masculine singular form of судить ('to judge'), here simple past meaning 'judged' or 'formed an opinion.' The verb takes the prepositional complement with о + человеке ('about/concerning a man'), so 'судил о человеке' = 'judged a person.'
segments.p1-11-g171: исповедовавшемся is a past active participle (from исповедоваться, reflexive 'to confess') agreeing in case, gender and number with человек; because человек appears in the prepositional after о, the participle takes the corresponding prepositional form ending -вшемся ('who had confessed'). перед публикой is a prepositional phrase where перед governs the instrumental (публикой = 'the public' in instrumental) meaning 'in the presence of / before the public.'
segments.p1-11-g217: Причин is the genitive plural of причина and precedes an impersonal existential construction. Может быть is an idiomatic, impersonal modal phrase meaning “may be/there can be,” and тысяча is a nominative numeral functioning as the notional subject; the whole order (genitive + может быть + numeral) yields “there may be a thousand reasons.”
segments.p1-11-g218: Но вот что ещё is a colloquial discourse formula introducing a new point: но = 'but/however', вот draws attention ('here/now'), что is the demonstrative/interrogative 'what/that', and ещё = 'more/another', so together: 'but here is one more thing' (often followed by a question or explanation).
segments.p1-11-g219: Для takes the genitive, so чего is the genitive form of что; the prepositional phrase для чего asks about purpose or motive and means 'for what' or 'for what purpose'.
segments.p1-11-g220: Зачем is a direct interrogative meaning 'what for/why'; собственно is an adverb adding 'actually/properly' (softening or tightening the question); я хочу писать is a present-tense statement of desire ('I want to write') with писать in the imperfective infinitive expressing the action intended. Together: 'what, actually, do I want to write?'.
segments.p1-11-g221: Если introduces a conditional clause ('if'); не negates the following prepositional phrase; для публики = 'for the public/for an audience' (публики is genitive after для). The clause means 'if not for the public'.
segments.p1-11-g222: Так = 'in that case/so'; ведь is a particle adding emphasis or 'after all'; можно is impersonal 'it is possible/one can'; бы is the conditional/modal particle (softening or making hypothetical); и так repeats 'that way/just so' for emphasis. Together they mean 'then, after all, one could just...' (i.e., it would be possible).
segments.p1-11-g223: Мысленно is an adverb 'mentally/in the mind'; все is the pronoun 'everything' as the object; припомнить is a perfective infinitive 'to recall/remember' (completed recollection). The phrase means 'to recall everything in one’s mind'.
segments.p1-11-g224: Не переводя is a negative gerund (деепричастие) from переводить meaning 'without translating/transferring'; на бумагу indicates the target 'onto paper' (на + accusative). The gerund expresses doing something without performing the action of putting it down on paper.
segments.p1-11-g225: Так‑с is an interjection/colloquial filler roughly 'well then' or 'so' used to pause or gather thought; it’s not a compositional phrase with literal meaning here (с appears as part of the colloquial tag).
segments.p1-11-g226: Но = 'but'; на бумаге uses the prepositional phrase to indicate medium ('on paper'); оно is a dummy/neuter pronoun referring to the matter just spoken of; выйдет is future of выйти used impersonally to mean 'will turn out/come out/appear'; как-то is a modal/qualifying adverb 'somehow/quite' softening the statement; торжественнее is the comparative form 'more solemn.' The clause means 'but on paper it will come out somehow more solemn.'
segments.p1-11-g183: Порядка и системы is a noun phrase in the genitive (singular) used as the object of the infinitive; in negative clauses Russian often uses the genitive after a negated verb. Заводить (infinitive) + не буду (future auxiliary) expresses a future refusal or decision: literally 'I will not be starting/establishing'. The object precedes the verb for emphasis, but the structure is equivalent to 'не буду заводить порядок и систему'.
segments.p1-11-g184: Что here is an indefinite/relative pronoun meaning 'what' or 'whatever' and heads a subordinate clause. Припомнится is a 3rd person singular reflexive (intransitive) verb in the future (perfective reflexive 'припомниться'), functioning impersonally: 'will occur/come to mind'.
segments.p1-11-g185: То is a demonstrative particle linking back to the previous clause ('then/that'); и is a small particle adding slight emphasis ('indeed/just' or 'also') before the verb. Запишу is the 1st person singular perfective future of записать: a straightforward promise/intention 'I will write down'.
segments.p1-11-g186: Ну вот is a colloquial discourse opener: Ну is an interjection ('well'), and вот is a deictic/introductory particle ('now/then' or 'well then') used to introduce the example that follows.
segments.p1-11-g187: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' and introduces the forthcoming illustration or hypothetical objection.
segments.p1-11-g188: Могли бы is the past-tense form of мочь plus the conditional particle бы, forming a polite hypothetical 'one could' or 'you might'. Придраться к слову is an infinitive phrase with придраться (perfective infinitive 'to pick a quarrel / nitpick') governed by к + dative (к слову, 'at the wording'). И спросить меня coordinates a second infinitive (спросить, perfective 'to ask') with the object меня in the accusative.
segments.p1-11-g189: Если introduces a conditional clause ('if'). Вы действительно не рассчитываете на читателей is a present-tense statement: рассчитываете на governs the accusative (на читателей) and means 'count on/expect'. Действительно is an adverb adding 'really/indeed', and не negates рассчитываете.
segments.p1-11-g190: То is the result/contrast particle ('then/so') linking to the preceding if-clause. Для чего (literally 'for what') asks the purpose; же is an emphatic particle that strengthens the question. Вы теперь делаете с самим собой is a present-tense clause: делать с собой means 'to do/make (something) with/for oneself' and самим (emphatic) + собой emphasizes 'to yourself.'
segments.p1-11-g191: Да еще is a colloquial intensifying phrase: да here functions like 'and'/'moreover' (somewhat archaic or emphatic), and ещё adds 'still/even/more' — together 'and what's more / even'. На бумаге is a simple prepositional phrase 'on paper' (на + noun in prepositional).
segments.p1-11-g192: Такие уговоры is a noun phrase meaning 'such assurances/promises/arguments': такие is a demonstrative adjective 'such', and уговоры (plural) here means verbal assurances or pledges one makes (more like 'assurances' than formal contracts).
segments.p1-11-g193: То есть is a fixed explanatory phrase 'that is' or 'that is to say' (то + есть). Что introduces the content clause that follows. Порядка и системы is in the genitive (negative object) and заводить не будете uses the infinitive заводить with the future auxiliary будете plus negation не: 'you will not set up/establish'. The word order places the infinitive before the auxiliary for stylistic emphasis (rather than the more neutral не будете заводить).
segments.p1-11-g194: Что again introduces another content clause. Запишете is the 2nd person plural future perfective of записать 'you will write down'; the postposed particle то forms the correlative construction то ... что ('that ... which'). The unusual placement (verb before то) is a stylistic choice, but the sense is 'that you will write (that)'.
segments.p1-11-g195: Что introduces the relative clause 'which ...'. Припомнится is 3rd person singular reflexive future (припомниться) 'will come to mind / be recalled' and completes the correlative: 'that you will write that which comes to mind.'
segments.p1-11-g196: И т. is an abbreviation equivalent to 'и так далее' ('and so on' / 'etc.'). И is the conjunction 'and', and т. is an abbreviated form used here to indicate continuation ('etc.').
segments.p1-11-g197: д. is an editorial/typographical abbreviation (here printed alone) typically used as part of 'и т. д.' ('и так далее' = 'and so on'). It functions as a shorthand marker meaning 'etc.' rather than a full lexical word; it behaves like punctuation/ellipsis in the text.
segments.p1-11-g246: Наконец is an adverbial discourse marker meaning "finally" or "in conclusion." Here it stands alone with a colon to mark the speaker's summing-up or decision; it does not affect verb tense.
segments.p1-11-g247: мне скучно is an impersonal predication: мне is a dative pronoun functioning as the experiencer ('to me'), and скучно is the short/neuter adverbial/predicative form meaning 'it is boring' or 'I am bored.' The construction literally reads 'to me (it is) boring.'
segments.p1-11-g248: а is a contrastive conjunction 'but/and' introducing opposition; я is the nominative subject 'I'; постоянно is an adverb 'constantly/always'; ничего не делаю is a negative clause where ничего ('nothing') combines with the negation particle не and the verb делаю (1st person present) to mean 'I do nothing' (emphatic negation). Word order places ничего before не for emphasis, but the meaning is standard negation.
segments.p1-11-g249: This whole clause is a verbless statement built around the deverbal noun Записыванье (an older/spelling-variant nominalization meaning 'the act of writing/recording'). же is an emphatic particle adding contrast ('though/however'), действительно is an adverb 'indeed/really,' and как будто introduces a simile 'as if/as though' that predicates работа ('work'). The sentence lacks an explicit copula—typical in Russian colloquial statements—so the sequence functions like 'The act of writing, however, really seems like work.'
segments.p1-11-g250: Говорят is an impersonal, third-person plural present indicative 'they say' used to introduce a general saying or opinion; there is no explicit subject (it corresponds to English 'they say' or 'people say').
segments.p1-11-g251: от работы uses the preposition от + genitive to indicate cause ('from/because of work'); человек is the nominative subject 'a person'; добрым и честным are in the instrumental case (dobrym, chestnym) because делается ('become', reflexive/passive-like) takes a predicate in the instrumental to express the resulting state. Делается is present tense of делаться meaning 'become' or 'get to be.'
segments.p1-11-g252: Ну is an interjection 'well' introducing the remark; вот is a discourse particle drawing attention to the fact (here functioning like 'well/now'); шанс is the nominative noun 'chance'; по крайней мере is a fixed adverbial idiom meaning 'at least' (po + adjectival phrase forms a frozen expression). The phrase as a whole is a short, colloquial comment: 'Well, there's a chance, at least.'
segments.p1-11-g253: Нынче is an adverb ('today/nowadays') placed at the front for temporal emphasis; идет is present tense, 3rd person singular of идти used impersonally for precipitation (here 'is falling' = 'is snowing'); снег is the grammatical subject in nominative. The verb-before-subject order (идет снег) is a normal Russian pattern for weather verbs.
segments.p1-11-g254: почти is an adverb meaning 'almost' modifying the following adjective; мокрый is an adjective in nominative masculine singular agreeing with снег and means 'wet' (here implying partially melted or slushy snow).
segments.p1-11-g255: желтый is another adjective in the same series agreeing with снег (nominative masculine singular); here it describes the snow's color/appearance ('yellow').
segments.p1-11-g256: мутный is the final adjective in the coordinate series modifying снег, also in nominative masculine singular; it means 'murky' or 'muddy' and completes the descriptive list.
segments.p1-11-g257: Вчера is an adverb of time ('yesterday'/'recently') placed before the verb for emphasis; шел is past tense, masculine singular of идти used impersonally for precipitation ('was falling' = 'was snowing'); тоже means 'also' and links this occurrence to the present one.
segments.p1-11-g258: на днях is an idiomatic time expression meaning 'the other day' or 'recently': на is the preposition used with дни (днях is prepositional plural) to form that phrase; тоже повторяет 'also'; шел again is past-tense идти used for precipitation. The clause is elliptical—the subject (снег) is omitted but understood from context.
segments.p1-11-g259: Мне is the dative pronoun ('to me') used with кажется to form the impersonal phrase 'it seems to me'; кажется is present tense of казаться and here means 'seems (to someone),' taking the dative for the experiencer.
segments.p1-11-g260: This is the main clause: я (nominative) is the subject; по поводу + noun (мокрого снега) is a prepositional phrase meaning 'on the subject of' or 'because of/about' the wet snow; припомнил is past tense perfective 'recalled' (direct object: тот анекдот); и here is a linking particle meaning 'and' (colloquial emphasis). Adjectival мокрого agrees in genitive masculine singular with снега because of the governing preposition/phrase.
segments.p1-11-g261: который introduces a relative clause modifying 'тот анекдот'; не хочет is present-tense 'does not want' and combines with the infinitive отвязаться ('to free oneself / to stop clinging') to form the idiom 'won't leave me alone'; от + меня is the preposition + genitive indicating separation from 'me'; теперь 'now' places the time frame.
segments.p1-11-g262: Итак is a discourse particle meaning 'thus' or 'so then,' used to introduce a conclusion or decision.
segments.p1-11-g263: пусть is the optative/imperative particle 'let' (here 'let it be'); это is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it'; будет is future/present-form of быть used here in the formula 'пусть ... будет' meaning 'let it be'; повесть is the predicate noun 'a story/tale' in nominative; по поводу + мокрого снега is the same prepositional phrase as before meaning 'about/regarding the wet snow.'
segments.p1-11-g239: 'А' is a contrastive conjunction here meaning 'but'. 'А между тем' is an idiomatic temporal phrase meaning 'meanwhile' or 'in the meantime'. 'Надобно' is an impersonal modal predicate ('it is necessary' / 'one must'), followed by the infinitive 'отвязаться' which expresses what is necessary. 'От него' is a prepositional phrase (от + genitive) indicating the thing to be shaken off; 'отвязаться' is reflexive (-ся) meaning 'to free oneself / to shake off'. Word order places the temporal phrase first, then the impersonal predicate, then the infinitive clause with its object.
segments.p1-11-g240: 'Таких' is the genitive plural form of 'такой' agreeing with 'воспоминаний' and means 'such' (qualifying the memories). 'Воспоминаний' is genitive plural ('memories'). 'У меня' is the Russian possession construction literally 'by me' used to mean 'I have'. 'Сотни' here is a predicative noun meaning 'hundreds' (i.e. there are hundreds of such memories). Overall: 'Of such memories, I have hundreds.'
segments.p1-11-g241: 'Но' is the adversative conjunction 'but'. 'По временам' is a temporal adverbial phrase meaning 'from time to time / occasionally' formed with по + dative plural. 'Из сотни' literally 'out of the hundred(s)' (из + genitive) means 'out of those hundreds'. 'Выдается' (выдаваться) here is present 3rd person singular passive/inchoative meaning 'comes out / stands out'. 'Одно какое-нибудь' is an indefinite subject phrase: 'одно' (one) with 'какое-нибудь' ('some, some sort of') modifying it. Finally 'и давит' is a coordinate clause: 'and it presses/weights upon (me)'; 'давит' is 3rd person singular present and here implies that the memory oppresses the speaker (the object 'me' is omitted but understood).
segments.p1-11-g242: 'Я' is the subject pronoun 'I'. 'Почему-то' is a compound adverb formed from 'почему' + the enclitic particle '-то', giving the sense 'for some reason' or 'somehow' (the particle makes the reason indefinite). 'Верю' is 1st person singular present of 'верить' meaning 'I believe'. The comma marks the start of the subordinate content clause introduced next.
segments.p1-11-g243: 'Что' introduces the content clause ('that ...'). 'Если' is the conditional 'if'. 'Я' is the subject 'I'. 'Его' is the accusative (here referring back to 'it' — the memory/motif) and is the direct object of 'запишу'. 'Запишу' is the perfective verb 'записать' in the simple future (1st person singular): 'I will write down' or 'I write down (and thereby complete the action)'. Together: 'that if I write it down...'.
segments.p1-11-g244: 'То' is the correlative/resultative particle paired with 'если ... то' and here means roughly 'then'. 'Оно' is the neuter pronoun 'it' referring back to the memory. 'И' here functions as a slight intensifier/connector ('and' / 'indeed') before the verb. 'Отвяжется' is future 3rd person singular of the reflexive verb 'отвязаться' meaning 'will come untied / will detach itself / will be shaken off' — i.e. the memory will stop bothering the speaker.
segments.p1-11-g245: 'Отчего' is an interrogative adverb meaning 'why' or 'for what reason'. The particle 'ж' (colloquial/enclitic) adds emphasis—'after all' or a mild insistence in the question. 'Не' is the negation particle used here with the infinitive to form the rhetorical 'why not ...' construction. 'Испробовать' is the infinitive 'to try out / to test'. Together the line is a rhetorical question: 'Why not try (it)?'
segments.p1-11-g227: В этом (preposition в + pronoun это in the prepositional case) is a locative frame meaning "in this" or "about this." Есть is the existential present verb "there is/there exists." Что-то is the indefinite pronoun "something," and внушающее is the present active participle of внушать used adjectivally (neuter to agree with что-то) meaning "impressive/suggestive." Together: "There is something impressive in this."
segments.p1-11-g228: Суда here is the genitive of суд used in a phrase meaning "judgment" (literally "judgment of/over"); больше is a comparative quantifier "more." Над собой is a prepositional phrase with над + instrumental/self-pronoun meaning "over/about oneself." Будет is the 3rd-person singular future of быть, giving an impersonal/future sense: "there will be more judgment over oneself." Word order places the verb at the end for emphasis.
segments.p1-11-g229: Слогу is the dative singular of слог (here meaning "style/turn of phrase"); прибавится is the reflexive verb прибавиться in the future tense, impersonal/semipredictive meaning "will be added to / will increase for." The dative marks what receives the addition: "the style will gain (something)."
segments.p1-11-g230: Кроме того is a fixed phrase (preposition + demonstrative genitive) meaning "besides/aside from that" or "moreover." It's a discourse connector introducing an additional point.
segments.p1-11-g231: Может быть is an idiomatic modal phrase formed from может (3rd person present of мочь) + быть (infinitive) meaning "maybe/perhaps." Functionally it marks possibility rather than a literal ability.
segments.p1-11-g232: Я is the nominative subject "I." От + noun in genitive (от записывания) means "from/through writing down/recording." Действительно is an adverb "indeed/actually." Получу is the perfective future 1st-person singular of получить "I will receive/get." Облегчение is the direct object "relief." The clause means "perhaps I will actually get relief from writing it down."
segments.p1-11-g233: Вот is a discourse particle meaning here/now/well used to draw attention; нынче is an adverb meaning "now/right now/nowadays." Together they set the temporal frame: "just now" or "at present."
segments.p1-11-g234: Например is an adverb meaning "for example" or "for instance," introducing an illustrative case.
segments.p1-11-g235: Меня is the accusative of я ("me") and here marks the person experiencing the pressure. Особенно is an adverb "especially/particularly." Давит is the 3rd-person singular present of давить used metaphorically "presses/oppresses." Одно давнишнее воспоминание is the nominative subject phrase "one long-ago/old memory" (одно = 'one' used to introduce the particular memory), placed after the verb and object for stylistic emphasis: "one old memory presses on me especially."
segments.p1-11-g236: Припомнилось is the neuter past form of the reflexive verb припомниться, meaning "came to mind/was recalled"; neuter agrees with the anticipatory ono (оно). Оно is the dummy/neuter pronoun "it." Мне is the dative "to me." Ясно is an adverb "clearly." Ещё на днях is an idiom meaning "just the other day/a few days ago" (еще adds recency; на днях = recently). И с тех пор is a time phrase "and since then" (с + genitive тех пор). Осталось is past tense of остаться "remained/stayed" (neuter to agree with оно). Со мною is the older/literary variant of с мной = "with me." Together: "It came to my mind clearly just a few days ago, and since then it has remained with me."
segments.p1-11-g237: Как introduces a simile "like/as." Досадный is an adjective "annoying/irksome." Музыкальный is an adjective "musical," and мотив is the noun "motif/theme" (here explicitly a musical motif). The whole phrase functions as a comparison: "like an annoying musical motif."
segments.p1-11-g238: Который is the relative pronoun "which/that," agreeing with мотив (masculine singular). Не is negation. Хочет is 3rd-person singular present of хотеть "wants," and отвязаться is the perfective reflexive infinitive "to free oneself / to shake off / to stop clinging"; together the clause means "which won't let itself be shaken off" or idiomatically "that won't let go / refuses to leave."
segments.p1-11-g137: "Но есть," is an existential phrase: "но" = 'but/however' introduces contrast, and "есть" is the copula used existentially ('there are' / 'exist'). Together they mean 'but there are'.
segments.p1-11-g138: "наконец," is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'finally/after all' (here softening or summing up what follows); comma shows it's an inserted remark before the noun phrase.
segments.p1-11-g139: "и такие," — "и" here has the sense 'also/even', and "такие" is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'such (ones)' (referring to things just mentioned). The phrase points forward to a class of things.
segments.p1-11-g140: This is a relative clause: "которые" ('which/that') refers back to "такие"; "даже и" is a doubled particle used for emphasis ('even'); "себе" is dative/reflexive 'to oneself'; "человек" here is an indefinite 'a person'; "открывать" is the imperfective infinitive 'to reveal/to disclose'; "боится" is present tense 'is afraid (to)'. Word order places "себе человек" together for stylistic emphasis ('a person even fears to reveal to himself').
segments.p1-11-g141: "и таких вещей у всякого порядочного человека довольно-таки накопится." — "и" = 'and'; "таких вещей" = 'of such things' (genitive plural); possession is expressed with "у + genitive": "у всякого порядочного человека" = 'every decent person has'; "довольно-таки" is an adverbial intensifier meaning 'quite/rather'; "накопится" is perfective future 3rd sg ('will accumulate' / 'will be accumulated'). The clause means every decent person will accumulate quite a number of such matters.
segments.p1-11-g142: "То есть даже так:" is an introductory comment: "то есть" = 'that is to say' (fixed explanatory phrase, where "есть" functions as a copula inside the phrase), and "даже так" = 'even so/like this', the colon signals that an explanation follows.
segments.p1-11-g143: "чем более он порядочный человек," is the first half of a correlative comparative construction "чем... тем...": "чем более" = 'the more...'; "он" is the subject; "порядочный человек" is a nominal phrase 'a decent person' (predicate in the comparative clause).
segments.p1-11-g144: "тем более у него их и есть." completes the correlative: "тем более" = 'the more (then)'; "у него" expresses possession ('he has'); "их" = 'them' (referring back to the things); the particle "и" here adds emphasis ('indeed/also'); "есть" is the copula/existential ('there are' / 'he has'). Together: 'the more decent he is, the more of them he has.'
segments.p1-11-g145: "По крайней мере," is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'at least' / 'in any case'; it is formed with the preposition "по" + genitive phrase "крайней мере" ('extreme measure') and functions as a discourse hedge/limiter.
segments.p1-11-g146: "я сам только недавно решился припомнить иные мои прежние приключения," — "я сам" is emphatic 'I myself'; "только недавно" = 'only recently'; "решился" (past reflexive) + infinitive "припомнить" = 'decided to recall'; "иные мои прежние приключения" is a stacked modifier sequence ('other/my/former adventures') with typical Russian ordering (adjective/pronoun + possessive + adjective + noun).
segments.p1-11-g147: "а до сих пор всегда обходил их," — "а" = contrast 'but/whereas'; "до сих пор" is a fixed temporal expression 'until now'; "всегда обходил" uses the imperfective past "обходил" to indicate a habitual or repeated past action ('always avoided'); "их" = 'them' (accusative plural).
segments.p1-11-g148: "даже с каким-то беспокойством." — "даже" = 'even'; "с" + instrumental "каким-то беспокойством" = 'with some kind of anxiety' — "каким-то" is the indefinite form ('some/what of a'), and the whole phrase modifies the verb "обходил" (he avoided them even with a certain unease).
segments.p1-11-g149: "Теперь же," — "теперь" = 'now'; the particle "же" adds emphasis or immediacy ('now then' / 'and now'). It sets the time frame for what follows.
segments.p1-11-g150: "когда я не только припоминаю," — "когда" = 'when/now that'; "я" subject; "не только" = 'not only'; "припоминаю" present imperfective 'I recall' (shows present/ongoing remembering).
segments.p1-11-g151: "но даже решился записывать," — "но" = 'but'; "даже" = 'even' (emphatic); "решился" (past reflexive) + infinitive "записывать" (imperfective) = 'decided/dared to begin writing down' — imperfective infinitive implies the act/process of writing down, not a single completed result.
segments.p1-11-g152: "теперь я именно хочу испытать:" — "теперь" repeats 'now', "я" subject, "именно" = 'precisely/exactly' adds force, "хочу испытать" = 'I want to test/try' (infinitive completion follows, introduced by colon).
segments.p1-11-g153: "можно ли хоть с самим собой совершенно быть откровенным и не побояться всей правды?" — "можно ли" = 'is it possible/whether it is possible'; "хоть" here = 'even/at all' adding concessive emphasis; "с самим собой" uses the instrumental to mean 'with oneself' ('самим' emphasizes 'oneself'); "совершенно быть откровенным" = 'to be completely candid'; "и не побояться всей правды" = 'and not be afraid of the whole truth' ("побояться" is perfective and governs genitive "правды"). The whole is a direct question about the possibility of radical self-honesty.
